Marketing

Best website builders for therapists: compared and ranked

Last updated: January 15, 2025 • 6 min read

# Best website builders for therapists: compared and ranked You’ve finished your clinical notes and now you need a website that doesn’t look like it was built in 2003. You want something that loads fast, keeps client data safe, and won’t eat your weekends. Below I’ve road-tested the three platforms therapists ask about most—Squarespace, Wix, and WordPress—so you can pick one and get back to seeing clients. > **Quick verdict:** Squarespace gives you the best balance of polished design and low maintenance. If you want drag-and-drop simplicity without sacrificing professionalism, start there. > Try SQUARESPACE free → ## Squarespace ### Pricing US $16–$52/mo | UK £15–£38/mo | AU $22–$58/mo | CA $20–$48/mo No free tier, but you can trial for 14 days. ### Key features you’ll actually use - HIPAA-compliant intake forms via third-party widgets (Jotform, Formstack) - Built-in SEO tools—edit slugs, meta titles, alt tags without a plugin - Therapy-specific templates: “Noll” and “Paloma” look clean and calming - Mobile-responsive out of the box - SSL certificate included (saves you $10–$20/yr) - Acuity scheduling baked into the Business tier—clients book and pay in one flow ### Pros - Drag-and-drop editor is truly zero code; move blocks, tweak fonts, done - Page-load speeds beat Wix and most cheap WordPress hosts on Google Core Web Vitals - 24/7 live chat + email support—no ticket queues - GDPR-compliant cookie banner toggle for UK and EU clients ### Cons - No native client portal; you’ll embed SimplePractice or TherapyNotes portals instead - Payment processing limited to Stripe, PayPal, Square—no lesser-known gateways - Templates are gorgeous but rigid; you can’t add deep custom code without a developer ### Best for Therapists who want a beautiful, low-maintenance brochure site with basic booking and payment. Try SQUARESPACE free → ## Wix ### Pricing US $17–$159/mo | UK £15–£119/mo | AU $21–$189/mo | CA $20–$179/mo Free tier available if you want to poke around before committing. ### Key features you’ll actually use - Wix Ascend CRM: tag leads, automate follow-up emails, track client journey - HIPAA-compliant forms on the Business Elite plan via the Wix Health app - 800+ templates including a ready-made “Mental Health Counsellor” layout - Wix Bookings + Zoom integration—send automatic session links - Multilingual support if you see clients in the UK or AU regions - Marketing automation: email and SMS drip campaigns built in ### Pros - Most flexible drag-and-drop design—move anything anywhere - App market is huge: Hotjar, Google Analytics, social media feeds, testimonials slider - Phone callback support on paid tiers—handy when you’re stuck - Free tier lets you test the editor before paying ### Cons - Once you publish, you can’t switch templates without rebuilding pages - Site speed is slower than Squarespace and optimized WordPress - HIPAA compliance only on the top-tier Business Elite plan—pricey if you just need forms ### Best for Therapists who want maximum design freedom and integrated marketing tools. Try WIX free → ## WordPress (self-hosted) ### Pricing Hosting: US $5–$25/mo | UK £4–£20/mo | AU $7–$30/mo | CA $6–£28/mo Themes/plugins: $0–$200/yr depending on how fancy you get. ### Key features you’ll actually use - Full ownership—move hosts, clone sites, export everything - Thousands of therapy themes (Astra, Divi, OceanWP) - HIPAA-compliant hosts: Convesio, HIPAA Vault, Atlantic.net - Plugin ecosystem: Amelia for booking, WPForms HIPAA for secure intake, Tutor LMS if you ever sell courses - Advanced SEO plugins: RankMath or Yoast give you schema, sitemaps, redirection - Multilingual with WPML or Polylang—useful for Canadian bilingual sites ### Pros - Cheapest long-term cost—pay only for hosting after year one - Unlimited scalability—add memberships, forums, directories later - Best raw SEO performance when optimized (you control every byte) - Can integrate with any EHR via API or webhooks—no gatekeepers ### Cons - Steeper learning curve—updates, backups, security fall on you or your developer - Hosting must be explicitly HIPAA-compliant; cheap shared hosts won’t cut it - Plugin conflicts happen; you may need to troubleshoot PHP errors ### Best for Therapists comfortable with tech or who hire developers and want maximum control and SEO power. Try WORDPRESS free → ## Head-to-head comparison | Criteria | Squarespace | Wix | WordPress | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Starting price (USD/mo) | $16 | $17 | $5 | | HIPAA-compliant forms | Via widget (extra cost) | Yes on Business Elite | Yes with HIPAA host + plugin | | Built-in booking | Yes (Acuity) | Yes | Via plugin (Amelia, BookingPress) | | SEO flexibility | Good | Good | Excellent | | Ease of use (1–5) | 5 | 4.5 | 3 | | Support quality | 24/7 chat/email | Callback + chat | Community/hosting dependent | ## Who should choose which - **Choose Squarespace** if you want to launch this weekend, hate tech, and need a site that looks like you paid a designer. - **Choose Wix** if you plan to run email campaigns, sell packages, or want pixel-level control over every section. - **Choose WordPress** if you’re on a tight budget long-term, need advanced SEO, or want to own your data and integrate with any future EHR. ## FAQ ### What is the easiest website builder for therapists with no tech skills? Squarespace. The editor is visual, support is 24/7, and you never touch code. ### Can I make a Wix site HIPAA compliant without the most expensive plan? No—you need the Business Elite tier plus the Wix Health app for HIPAA forms. ### Is WordPress safe for private-practice therapy websites? Yes, but only if you choose a HIPAA-compliant host and use plugins designed for HIPAA, such as WPForms HIPAA or Amelia on encrypted servers. ### Which builder is best for UK and Australian therapists needing GDPR and cookie consent? Squarespace and Wix both include GDPR banners; WordPress needs a plugin like Complianz. ### How much does it really cost to run a therapy website on WordPress per year? Expect roughly $60–$300 for hosting + $0–$200 for premium themes/plugins—still cheaper than Wix Business Elite after year one. ## Conclusion Pick Squarespace for the fastest professional launch, Wix if you want built-in marketing automation, or WordPress for ultimate control and lower long-term cost. Start your free trial today and get your first client booking within the week. Try SQUARESPACE free →
